name: operator-local-codex-git-workflow
description: Operator-grade local git-backed coding workflow using Codex in ~/dev/temp with paired shell and interactive review turns.

steps.label: prepare_codex_workspace
steps.prompt: Please prepare a Codex workspace in ~/dev/temp/con-bench-codex-git by launching Codex there and preparing a paired local shell target for file, git, and test work.

steps.label: bootstrap_project_and_commit
steps.prompt: Keep the Codex target prepared, but use its paired local shell target to create a tiny Python project in ~/dev/temp/con-bench-codex-git with mathops.py and test_mathops.py, initialize git, make an initial commit, and run `python3 -m unittest -q`. Only touch the Codex pane if a blocking trust or continue prompt must be cleared first.

steps.label: feature_change_and_diff
steps.prompt: Still in the same workspace pair, add a new pairwise_sum helper to mathops.py, update the tests, rerun `python3 -m unittest -q`, then show `git status --short` and `git diff --stat` from the paired local shell.

steps.label: interactive_review_turn
steps.prompt: Ask Codex in the existing Codex pane for a brief review summary of the current diff and a one-line commit message proposal. Do not create a new target.

steps.label: summarize_continuity
steps.prompt: Summarize which target handled shell work, which target handled the Codex review turn, and confirm the workspace path.
